title: Officials: Man who shot 2 in Hampton Beach before dying by suicide was active-duty Navy sailor
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tyshawn Cooper, a 28-year-old active-duty Navy sailor, shot two people in Hampton Beach, New Hampshire, before dying by suicide.
The shooting victims, a 23-year-old man and a 25-year-old woman, are hospitalized as of Monday afternoon, officials said.
Cooper was an Information Systems Technician on the USS Hampton, undergoing maintenance at Portsmouth Naval Shipyard in Kittery, Maine.

title: Report: Celtics’ Paul George turned down $3.9 million trade bonus
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Paul George declined a $3.9 million trade bonus, keeping his Boston Celtics cap hit at $54.1 million.
The Celtics are $30 million over the cap, and George's decision aids their cap management efforts.
Boston aims to avoid second apron penalties, following recent trades of Jrue Holiday and Kristaps Porzingis.

title: R.I. food bank thanks Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce for pre-wedding donation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donated $1 million to the Rhode Island Community Food Bank before their wedding.
The donation will help the food bank purchase and distribute nutritious food to 137 agencies across Rhode Island.
The couple also donated to several other charities, including major food banks in New York, Pennsylvania, and Kansas.

title: 5 takeaways from the Celtics’ press conference about the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and draft picks, discussing financial and strategic reasons for the move.
Brad Stevens emphasized seeking 'optionality' and cited adjustments required under the new NBA collective bargaining agreement.
Bill Chisholm addressed concerns about the Celtics' treatment of stars, reinforcing the franchise's commitment to player satisfaction.

title: 5 takeaways from the U.S. loss to Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The United States was defeated by Belgium 4-1 in the World Cup Round of 16 in Seattle.
Belgium's Charles De Ketelaere scored twice, with goals also from Hans Vanaken and Romelu Lukaku.
The U.S. displayed poor defense and sparse attacking, exiting the tournament despite being co-hosts.

title: 2 siblings drown in Vermont river, brother tried to save younger sister, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sandro Lala, 25, and Yazmin Yupangui, 11, drowned in Vermont's Lamoille River during a rescue attempt by Lala.
The siblings disappeared after Yapangui fell into the river; their bodies were found two days apart following a storm.
A GoFundMe raised nearly $70,000 to cover funeral costs and transport the siblings' remains to Ecuador for burial.

title: City declines to use reserve funds to prevent BPS layoffs as more than 560 positions are cut
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston declines to use reserve funds to prevent more than 560 Boston Public Schools layoffs due to budget constraints.
The $1.7 billion BPS budget, facing a 3,000 student enrollment drop, leads to over 400 layoffs.
City officials argue layoffs allow investment in high-quality academics and maintaining a 10:1 student-teacher ratio.

title: Harmful bacteria persist in Merrimack River days after Haverhill sewer break
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Harmful bacteria remain in Merrimack River over a week after a sewer break released untreated sewage.
Four out of seven testing sites report fecal coliform and E. coli levels above recreational safety limits.
Shellfish harvesting areas in several Massachusetts towns remain closed until water quality standards are met.

title: Boater remains missing after crash off of Salisbury Beach
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A boater remains missing after a crash into the jetty off Salisbury Beach late Friday night.
Two individuals were rescued and hospitalized post-crash near Salisbury Beach State Reservation at approximately 10:35 p.m.
Authorities, including State Police and the Coast Guard, suspended the search for missing boater Vilmar Dorner, 49, from North Reading.

title: After officiating Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding, Adam Sandler played pickup basketball in this Mass. vacation hotspot
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Adam Sandler officiated Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding at Madison Square Garden on Friday evening.
On Sunday, Sandler played pickup basketball in Nantucket, after seeking game info from local police.
Sandler has a habit of joining random basketball games and frequently takes photos with fellow players.
